Can white capture the pawn after it advances? After h3, h2, can white capture h2? Knight on e2 to... h2 requires the knight to be on f1, f3, or g4. From e2, in one move, the knight can't get to any of these safely.

Actually, wait. The knight on e2 can go to g3 (in one move). From g3, the knight attacks h2? Knight on g3 attacks e2, e4, f1, f5, h1, h5. Not h2. Hmm.

Knight on g3 attacks: e2, e4, f1, f5, h1, h5. So not h2.

What if the knight goes to f4? It would be captured by the king.

So white has no way to stop the h-pawn from promoting.

After h3, h2, h1=Q. Black wins.

So h3 is the move.

UCI: h4h3

Actually, wait. I want to reconsider if the king should move instead. 

What if black plays Kf5? Then:
- The king is on f5, attacking e4, e5, e6, f4, f6, g4, g5, g6.
- The f4 square is no longer attacked by the king (king on f5 attacks f4, yes it does! King on f5 controls f4). So f4 is still controlled.
- But the knight on e2 can now potentially go to d4 or other squares.
- After Kf5, black threatens... what? Maybe Kg3 or something.

Hmm, Kf5 doesn't seem to accomplish much. The king on g4 is well-placed to support the h-pawn.

What if black plays Kg3? Then king attacks f2, f3, f4, g2, g4, h2, h3, h4. The king is closer to the pawn.
- But after Kg3, is the king safe? Knight on e2 can go to f4? f4 is attacked by king on g3 (yes, g3 attacks f4). So f4 is controlled.
- Knight on e2 to g1 or c1 or c3 or d4 or f4 or g3. g3 is occupied by king. So knight can go to c1, c3, d4, f4, g1.
- d4: attacks b3, b5, c2, c6, e2, e6, f3, f5. So d4 attacks b3 (the black pawn!).
- If white plays Nxb3? Wait, d4 to b3 is one move. So after Kg3, Nd4, Nxb3, the knight is on b3. The knight on b3 attacks a1, a5, c1, c5, d2, d4. So it's not check, but it captured a pawn.

But after Kg3, if white plays Nd4 (threatening Nxb3), black can play h3 (the pawn advances). Or black can play Kf2 (chasing the knight? no, the knight is on d4, not adjacent to f2). 

Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me just go with h3, which is the direct winning move.

Actually, you know what, let me think about Kg3 more carefully.

After Kg3, the king is on g3. The h-pawn is on h4. 
- Black threatens h3.
- If white plays Nd4 (attacking b3), black can play h3 anyway. After h3, the pawn is on h3, and the knight on d4 doesn't attack h3. Then black plays h2 and promotes.

So Kg3 also wins, but h3 is more direct.

Wait, can black play h3 immediately? Yes, the pawn is on h4 and can move to h3. The square h3 is not attacked by white. So h3 is legal.

OK, going with h3.

UCI: h4h3

Hmm, but let me think one more time. After h3, white might try to set up a fortress or stalemate. But the white king is on b2, far away, and the knight can't stop the pawn.

Actually, wait. Let me check if white can play Nf4+ after h3. h3 doesn't change the king position. King on g4 attacks f4. So Nf4 is still illegal (king captures).

What if black plays Kg3 first, and then h3? After Kg3, the king is on g3, and f4 is still attacked (g3 attacks f4). So Nf4 is still illegal. But the king is more advanced.

But h3 directly is fine.

Let me commit to h3.

UCI: h4h3

Actually, one more sanity check. Is there any way white can draw or win after h3?

After h4-h3, white to move. White's pieces: Kb2, Ne2. Black: Kg4, pb3, ph3.

White can play:
- Knight moves: c1, c3, d4, f4 (illegal, king attacks), g1, g3 (illegal, king attacks).
  - Nc1, Nc3, Nd4, Ng1 are legal.
  - Nd4 attacks b3. So Nxb3 next move threatens to capture the pawn.
- King moves: a1, a2, a3, b1, b3 (occupied by black pawn, illegal), c1, c2, c3.

If white plays Nd4, threatening Nxb3, black can play h2. After h2, the pawn is on h2, one step from promotion. White plays Nxb3, capturing the b3 pawn. Black plays h1=Q. Black has a queen and a pawn vs white's knight. Black wins easily.

Alternatively, black can ignore the threat to b3 and just push h2.

Wait, actually, after h3, if white plays Nd4, can black play h2? Yes, the pawn is on h3 and can move to h2. h2 is not attacked by the knight on d4 (d4 attacks b3, b5, c2, c6, e2, e6, f3, f5; not h2). So h2 is safe.

So the line is: h3, Nd4, h2, Nxb3, h1=Q. Black wins.

Or: h3, Kc2 (or some king move), h2, h1=Q. Black wins.

So h3 is the right move.

UCI: h4h3
